Index.title: FITA | Multidisciplinary execution for projects and works
Index.description: Execution company able to participate in multidisciplinary works and projects according to scope, stage, and technical requirements.

Footer.description: Multidisciplinary execution company for civil works, metal-mechanical work, installation and erection, and anticorrosive coatings, with capacity for partial or integral scopes.

Hero.headline: FITA executes multidisciplinary projects according to the real scope of each job.
Hero.subheadline: We participate in new construction, maintenance, expansion, or rehabilitation, taking integral projects or specific work packages in civil works, metal-mechanical work, installation and erection, and anticorrosive coatings for public and private clients.
Hero.panelTitle: We adapt to the scope, budget, and stage of each project
Hero.panelBullet1: We can participate in a specific package or in a broader execution scope
Hero.panelBullet2: We integrate civil, metal-mechanical, installation, and coating capabilities as needed
Hero.panelBullet3: When required, we add specialist support without overstating the scope we directly control

Overview.title: One company for different execution fronts
Overview.description1: Construcción y Mantenimientos FITA S.A. de C.V. is an execution company with documented experience in industrial, metal-mechanical, and civil projects. We are not positioned as a single-trade contractor, but as a company that enters with the right scope for each job.
Overview.description2: We work from bids, invitations, direct awards, and client requirements. If the project needs a broader front, we structure it. If it only needs a defined package, we participate with clear scope and responsibility.

Institutional.standardsDesc: FITA’s priority is to understand exactly which part of the project must be executed and under what responsibility. That clarity allows us to participate without overpromising or losing operational control.
Institutional.sectorsDesc: Our track record combines industrial, metal-mechanical, and civil work. That is why the main site presents a global company profile rather than a single-specialty contractor.
Institutional.collabDesc: FITA can execute against client engineering when it exists, but also according to the bid scope, project budget, stage, and technical requirements that define each opportunity.

Model.title: Adaptive operating model
Model.subtitle: Each project defines the team and the depth of scope.
Model.description1: FITA is not presented as a rigid structure. We build execution capacity according to the type of work, the size of the front, the dominant discipline, and the real contracting conditions.
Model.description2: That allows us to participate from a single package to a broader execution scope. And when a highly specific specialty is needed—such as radiography, penetrant testing, soil mechanics, or topography—we integrate external specialists without losing overall coordination.

Capabilities.title: Core capabilities
Capabilities.subtitle: Four base lines for the global site, while specific products and services can live in dedicated subdomains.

items.title: Civil works
items.description: Participation in new construction, maintenance, expansion, or rehabilitation for fronts such as schools, classrooms, sports courts, guardhouses, foundations, finishes, waterproofing, and other civil work according to scope.

items.title: Metal-mechanical and structural work
items.description: Fabrication, preparation, erection, welding, plate work, piping, and related work for metallic and industrial infrastructure according to project requirements.

items.title: Installation and erection
items.description: Integration and installation of components, equipment, or execution packages, including electromechanical or electrical fronts when they are part of the contracted scope.

items.title: Anticorrosive coatings
items.description: Surface preparation and application of anticorrosive and protective systems for exposed or industrial infrastructure.

Projects.title: Experience that can support a real conversation
Projects.subtitle: Documented work types and references to help clients understand where FITA fits.

items.title: Offshore platforms and marine fronts
items.description: The legacy CV documents participation on Arenque platforms with structural panels, risers, pipe defenses, dismantling, process lines, handrails, and grating work.

items.title: Metal-mechanical fabrication and erection
items.description: Experience in structural fabrication, piping assemblies, process valves, qualified welding, and erection of structural and operational elements.

items.title: Surface protection and coating systems
items.description: Background in sandblasting, anticorrosive systems, and protective work for industrial infrastructure and helipad surfaces.

items.title: Maintenance and modifications
items.description: Participation in corrective maintenance, reinforcements, dismantling, grating modifications, lifting maneuvers, and conditioning work for operation or intervention.

items.title: Institutional civil work
items.description: The documented history also includes domes, restroom rehabilitation, laboratories, guardhouses, and sports courts, which supports the civil line of the global site.

items.title: Partial or broader scopes
items.description: FITA can integrate into a specific package or take on a broader execution front depending on budget, bid structure, stage, and client responsibility requirements.

Safety.title: Field execution criteria
Safety.subtitle: The promise is not to sound big. The promise is to execute with order, safety, and clear scope responsibility.

items.title: Safety and operational control
items.description: Safety is part of how each work front is organized and executed.

items.title: Quality and scope compliance
items.description: Each package must match what is truly contracted, executed, and deliverable, avoiding gaps between sales language and field operations.

items.title: Environmental responsibility and jobsite order
items.description: Execution is approached with attention to order, cleanliness, responsible work practices, and applicable project requirements.

Methodology.title: How FITA enters a project
Methodology.subtitle: A clear path to participate without confusion about scope from day one.

steps.title: Opportunity review
steps.description: We analyze the bid, invitation, requirements, or need to determine whether FITA should participate in the full project or only in a defined package.

steps.title: Real scope definition
steps.description: We define what FITA will execute, what resources the front requires, and which external specialties may be needed if the project demands them.

steps.title: Operational planning
steps.description: We organize work front, personnel, sequence, supplies, timing, and responsibilities according to the stage and complexity of the job.

steps.title: Field or workshop execution
steps.description: We execute the contracted package or project in civil works, metal-mechanical work, installation and erection, or coatings according to the defined front.

steps.title: Control and coordination
steps.description: We monitor scope compliance, discipline coordination, and critical points related to safety, quality, and progress.

steps.title: Delivery and continuity
steps.description: We close the executed front and leave room for future stages, expansions, maintenance, or additional packages.

Procurement.title: Formal participation in projects
Procurement.description: FITA can join projects by bid, invitation, or direct award while keeping a clear conversation about scope, participation conditions, and the right contact channel for each case.
Procurement.bullet1: Email for invitations and formal contact
Procurement.bullet2: WhatsApp for quick questions or first contact
Procurement.bullet3: Flexibility for partial or broader proposals

Contact.title: Let’s talk about your project scope
Contact.subtitle: If you want to invite FITA to participate in a project, use email for formal contact. If you first need to solve a quick question, you can also reach us on WhatsApp.
Contact.name: Ing. Gustavo Bierge
Contact.address: Paseo de los Mexicas 126-D, 16 de Septiembre, Ciudad Madero, Tamaulipas, postal code 89512, Mexico.
Contact.coverage: FITA can participate in public or private projects, in partial or broader scopes, from Ciudad Madero toward different work fronts according to the project.
Contact.formalValue: Invitations, proposal requests, bid documents, or scope packages
Contact.quickValue: Initial questions, interest validation, or first contact through WhatsApp

Faq.title: The essentials to understand how FITA participates
Faq.subtitle: These answers are written for clients who need to know quickly whether FITA is a fit for their project.

items.title: What does FITA do?
items.description: FITA is a multidisciplinary execution company that can participate in civil works, metal-mechanical work, installation and erection, and anticorrosive coatings, either in a partial scope or in a broader project front.

items.title: Does FITA only work from client engineering?
items.description: Not necessarily. FITA can execute against client engineering when it exists, but also according to the bid scope, project stage, budget, and technical requirements of the contract.

items.title: Does FITA take full projects or only packages?
items.description: Both are possible. FITA can integrate into a specific package or a broader execution scope depending on the required responsibility and contracting format.

items.title: What types of work support FITA’s experience?
items.description: The documented history includes industrial and offshore work, metal-mechanical fabrication and erection, anticorrosive coatings, and institutional civil work such as domes, laboratories, guardhouses, and sports courts.

items.title: What is the best way to contact FITA?
items.description: For invitations, scope packages, or formal project contact, email cmfitasadecv@gmail.com. For quick questions or first contact, use WhatsApp at +52 833 518 1171.
